title: Apply an edited cleaning-map workbook to produce a cleaned .sav
summary: Reads the input data file and the edited cleaning-map .xlsx, applies the requested renames/recodes/missing marks, and writes a cleaned .sav to the output directory.
description: Each row in the cleaning map that has a non-empty `new value`, `new label`, or `mark-missing` cell is applied to the corresponding (variable, value) pair: value recodes rewrite the underlying integer code, new labels replace the value label, mark-missing adds the value to the variable's declared-missing range. Rows left blank are no-ops. When `include_vars` is supplied, only those variables are cleaned and the rest pass through unchanged.

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory for the cleaned .sav; created if missing.

arguments.name: include_vars
arguments.description: Restrict cleaning to this subset of variable names. Empty = apply to every variable present in the map.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Optional filename prefix for the cleaned output.

arguments.name: suffix
arguments.description: Optional filename suffix (before the extension) for the cleaned output.

notes: The cleaning map is the single source of truth — value collisions (e.g. two source codes mapping to the same target) are reported as errors, not silently merged.
When applying to CSV/Excel input, string columns participate in rename-only cleaning (no numeric recoding or missing-code declarations).

artifacts.title: Cleaned SPSS file
artifacts.description: The input data with all recodes, renames, and missing-value declarations from the cleaning map applied. Produced as an SPSS .sav file preserving variable labels, measure levels, and the corrected value codes.

details:
The cleaning map workbook — produced by `write-cleaning-map-template` — lists every (variable, value) pair observed in the input. Each row carries editable columns: `new label` (replaces the value label in the output .sav), `new value` (recodes the numeric code), and `mark-missing` (adds the value to the variable’s declared-missing range). Rows left blank are no-ops — only filled-in cells trigger changes.

Operations are applied in a specific order: renames first, then recodes, then missing-code declarations. Value collisions — two source codes that recode to the same target code — are reported as errors before any output is written, so a partial output is never produced. When `include_vars` is supplied, only those variables are passed through the cleaning logic; all other variables are written unchanged from the input.

The output is always a .sav file. If the input was a CSV or Excel file, apply-cleaning-map performs rename-only cleaning (no numeric recoding or missing-code declarations, since those require integer codes).

see_also.description: Generate the blank cleaning-map template that you edit and supply to this command.
Inspect the resulting .sav’s variable labels, value labels, and missing codes after cleaning.

examples.title: Apply a completed cleaning map to an SPSS file
